data.body:
PASS 1 (G6 — approach soundness, mechanism & alternative-selection). All correctness reasoning grounded in the live reducer/compaction code.

AFFIRMATIONS (criteria this chunk passes):
- G6 fitness-for-purpose (solves the named problem, not a proxy): the "Why" precisely diagnoses why the raw-history "ever-seen" approach (local_label_intent) fails CLOSED across compaction, and the fix targets exactly that. Verified: process_snapshot (src/rebar/reducer/_processors.py:515-519) blindly copies EVERY compiled_state key, so a managed_refs field in compiled_state "survives compact_ticket for free" is mechanically TRUE; compaction serializes via canonical_str sorted-keys (src/rebar/_commands/compact.py:11-12), so a deterministically-sorted list yields byte-parity.
- G6 approach selection: I generated two structurally-different alternatives — (a) recompute the ever-seen set on-demand by scanning raw event history (the local_label_intent shape), and (b) a sidecar provenance file/projection OUTSIDE compiled_state. (a) is the very approach the plan rejects with a grounded compaction-survival argument (compact.py:67-86 deletes the originals inside the lock, so any pre-snapshot removal is lost on re-derive). (b) is strictly worse on codebase-alignment + blast-radius (forfeits the existing process_snapshot/compaction/byte-parity machinery and adds reducer I/O the reducer avoids). Chosen approach is defensibly at-least-as-good on every axis — PASS; alternatives discarded (not written into the plan).
- G6 mechanism (concurrency/atomicity): the fold + migration-seeding run inside deterministic reducer replay (no concurrency at reduce time), so the "seed when absent" check-then-seed is NOT a TOCTOU. Strict reducer-side monotonicity (never pruned by UNLINK; process_unlink at :243-246 only shrinks deps) correctly keeps the resurrection window closed; deferring reclamation to a reconcile-time prune event avoids the prune-vs-fold hazard. Sound.
- G6 mechanism (empty/back-out): gate defaults missing managed_refs -> empty -> returns False (additive-only: no remove/clobber/churn), doubling as a safe no-op back-out. Well-specified fail-safe.
- G6 positive rationale: PRESENT and strong (compaction-survival, single provider-agnostic primitive, fail-safe default, explicit back-out posture). No rationale finding.

FINDING (G6 mechanism — the migration-seeding hook's PLACEMENT and ORDERING in the reduce path is under-specified, and it is load-bearing):
- [ ] Specify WHERE in the reduce path managed_refs seeding runs and that it executes as a post-replay finalization step (after the SNAPSHOT restore AND after all post-snapshot CREATE/LINK/parent-EDIT folds), not inside process_snapshot. process_snapshot (_processors.py:515-519) blindly copies compiled_state keys and would NOT seed an absent key; a pre-feature SNAPSHOT simply lacks managed_refs. If seeding ran at restore-time BEFORE a same-pass UNLINK, the seed-then-unlink ordering could mis-record; and process_edit's `if field_name not in state: continue` guard (_processors.py:328) would silently drop a managed_refs EDIT, so the field cannot be folded by the generic EDIT path either. The plan says seeding happens "on the FIRST reduce/restore where managed_refs is absent" but leaves the insertion point and its ordering relative to UNLINK/fold unspecified — the exact ordering assumption AC #2's "later unlink propagates" depends on.
  criteria: [G6]
  location: plan "Migration" section + AC #2 ("When managed_refs is absent ... the reducer seeds it from current deps + parent_id; a test proves ... its later unlink propagates")
  evidence: process_snapshot (_processors.py:515-519) is a blind key-copy with no seed branch; process_edit (:328) skips fields not already in state; the plan's "FIRST reduce/restore where managed_refs is absent" leaves the hook location and ordering vs. same-pass UNLINK unstated, an absence the plan text itself under-specifies.
  suggested_fix: State that seeding is a finalization pass over the fully-reduced state (current deps + parent_id) run after replay_events completes and after the monotonic folds, so a seeded pre-feature ticket's later UNLINK operates on an already-populated set; add the AC #2 test to compact under OLD code (no managed_refs key), then seed+unlink and assert propagation.
